title: Ecosystem Carbon Budget Calculator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive calculator for GPP, NPP, and NEP with five ecosystem types, adjustable parameters, and disturbance simulation

learningObjectives: Calculate GPP, NPP, and NEP for different ecosystem scenarios
Determine whether an ecosystem is a carbon sink or source
Predict how disturbances (fire, drought, warming) shift the carbon balance
Compare carbon budgets across major ecosystem types
prerequisites: Understanding of photosynthesis and cellular respiration
Familiarity with producer and decomposer concepts

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Calculator-based quantitative exploration with disturbance scenarios
keyQuestions: Can a healthy forest ever become a carbon source? Under what conditions?
Which ecosystem type has the largest carbon sink capacity and why?
How do fire, drought, and warming each affect the carbon budget differently?
What is the climate change feedback loop between warming, respiration, and carbon release?
Why is tundra permafrost thawing considered a climate tipping point?
commonMisconceptions: Forests always absorb CO2 (disturbed or old-growth forests can be carbon neutral or even sources)
NPP and NEP are the same thing (NEP also accounts for heterotrophic respiration by decomposers and animals)
Warming only affects photosynthesis (it actually increases respiration rates more than photosynthesis, potentially flipping sink to source)
assessmentOpportunities: Have students calculate NPP and NEP from given GPP, Ra, and Rh values
Ask students to determine the tipping point where a tropical forest switches from sink to source under warming
Challenge students to rank the five ecosystems by carbon sink capacity and explain the ecological reasons for differences
